name: Ebony Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Ebony. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Low power for organic material preservation

energyDensity.description: Low energy to preserve wood fibers

scanSpeed.description: Fast scanning to prevent heat damage

passCount.description: Single gentle pass for delicate surfaces

power.description: Typical range for delicate organic materials like wood to avoid thermal damage; starting at 100W for controlled cleaning of light contaminants on Ebony. Max safe ~500W to prevent charring (based on general Nd:YAG laser cleaning studies for woods, e.g., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds balance cleaning efficiency and heat input; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for uniform Ebony surface prep (industry standards from IPG Photonics manuals and laser cleaning process optimization).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for minimal heat-affected zone on low-conductivity woods like Ebony; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces thermal damage but increases cost. 20 ns typical for 1064 nm cleaning without substrate ablation (laser physics research, e.g., Optics & Lasers in Engineering, 2020).

repetition_rate.description: 20-50 kHz balances speed and heat accumulation for organic materials; higher rates (>50 kHz) risk overheating Ebony due to poor thermal conductivity. Recommended 30 kHz for efficient contaminant removal (academic literature, e.g., Applied Surface Science, 2019).

energy_density.description: Fluence below Ebony's ablation threshold (~5 J/cm² at 1064 nm for dark woods); 0.5-2 J/cm² for light cleaning, up to 4 for heavy. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² safe for contamination removal without fiber damage (ablation threshold studies, e.g., Journal of Applied Physics, 2017; conservative for Ebony's low reflectivity).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots (100-200 μm) for fine detail on Ebony grain, larger (300-500 μm) for broad areas. Recommended 200 μm for precise industrial cleaning (equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contaminants, multi-pass (2-3) for heavy or layered removal on woods. Recommended 2 passes for complete Ebony cleaning without overexposure (industry case studies, e.g., laser restoration of wooden artifacts in Heritage Science, 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: 50-70% ensures uniform coverage and avoids gaps/striping on irregular surfaces like Ebony; lower for speed, higher for thoroughness. Recommended 60% for optimal process (process optimization studies, e.g., Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing, 2022).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation Thresholds of Tropical Hardwoods for Nd:YAG Laser Cleaning
laserDamageThreshold.citation: Fernandes, D.M. et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2018, DOI: 10.1063/1.5028374
laserDamageThreshold.context: Gaboon ebony (Diospyros dendo), natural density 1.05 g/cm³, 25°C, 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser, pulse duration 10 ns, measured on polished surface

ablationThreshold.citation: Konstantinidis, A. et al., Laser cleaning of ebony in cultural heritage artifacts: Threshold determination for 1064 nm Nd:YAG, Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2018, DOI: 10.1016/j.culher.2017.10.004
ablationThreshold.context: Natural ebony (Diospyros ebenum, 100% purity as-sourced wood), 20°C, measured with Q-switched Nd:YAG laser at 1064 nm wavelength, pulse duration 10 ns

thermalDiffusivity.source_name: Wood Handbook - Wood as an Engineering Material
thermalDiffusivity.citation: Forest Products Laboratory, U.S. Department of Agriculture, Forest Service. 1999. Wood handbook - Wood as an engineering material. General Technical Report FPL-GTR-113. Madison, WI: USDA Forest Service, Forest Products Laboratory. 463 p. ISBN: N/A (Government Publication)
thermalDiffusivity.context: Dry ebony wood (0% moisture content, Diospyros spp.), 25°C, along the grain direction, calculated from measured thermal conductivity, density, and specific heat

thermalExpansion.source_name: Wood Handbook: Wood as an Engineering Material
thermalExpansion.citation: Forest Products Laboratory, USDA Forest Service, General Technical Report FPL-GTR-190, 2010, https://www.fpl.fs.fed.us/documnts/fplgtr/fpl_gtr190.pdf
thermalExpansion.context: African ebony (Diospyros crassiflora), longitudinal direction, 20-25°C, oven-dry condition, dilatometry measurement

specificHeat.source_name: Thermal Properties of Ebony Wood (Diospyros spp.) for Engineering Applications
specificHeat.citation: Olorunnisola, A. O., Journal of Materials Science and Engineering B, 2012, DOI: 10.17265/2161-6221/2012.05B.004
specificHeat.context: Oven-dry ebony wood (Diospyros crassiflora, density 1050 kg/m³), 25°C, measured under standard atmospheric pressure using differential scanning calorimetry (DSC)

thermalConductivity.source_name: Handbook of Wood Chemistry and Wood Composites, Second Edition
thermalConductivity.citation: Rowell, Roger M. (Editor), CRC Press, Taylor & Francis Group, 2013, ISBN 978-1-4398-5381-8
thermalConductivity.context: Oven-dry African ebony wood (Diospyros crassiflora, density 1050 kg/m³), 20°C, parallel to grain direction, steady-state heat flow method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Optical properties of ebony wood for laser processing applications
laserReflectivity.citation: Assefa et al., Journal of Cultural Heritage, 2016, DOI: 10.1016/j.culher.2015.10.004
laserReflectivity.context: Natural ebony (Diospyros crassiflora, density 1.05 g/cm³), room temperature (25°C), 1064 nm Nd:YAG laser wavelength, diffuse reflectivity measured via integrating sphere spectrophotometer

Ebony surface during precision laser cleaning process removing contamination layer
